growth_records.medium: Anaerobic minimal medium with acetate as electron donor and iodate (IO3-) as electron acceptor
growth_records.cite: Reyes-Umana et al. Genetic and phylogenetic analysis of dissimilatory iodate-reducing bacteria identifies potential niches across the world's oceans. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-01034-5. [PMC8692401]
growth_records.snippet: The doubling time of cells grown under these conditions is 10.96 h (µ = 0.06) which is roughly three times longer than cells growing under aerobic conditions in analogous media (3.42 h, µ = 0.20).
growth_records.notes: Both mu (0.06 h^-1) and doubling time (10.96 h) directly reported in text for anaerobic acetate/iodate-respiring growth. Medium recipe (per liter salts) taken from Methods 'minimal media' recipe; acetate (10 mM) and iodate (2 mM) concentrations taken from the routine passaging/growth protocol described in Methods (matches the reported optimum of 2 mM IO3-). Substrate consumption was also reported (2.46±0.499 mM IO3- consumed, 2.86±0.427 mM acetate oxidized, OD600 increase of 0.109) but no incubation time is stated in the text, so a specific uptake/secretion rate (per gDW per h) could not be calculated.

growth_records.medium: Analogous minimal medium with acetate as electron donor and O2 as electron acceptor (aerobic)
growth_records.cite: Reyes-Umana et al. Genetic and phylogenetic analysis of dissimilatory iodate-reducing bacteria identifies potential niches across the world's oceans. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-01034-5. [PMC8692401]
growth_records.snippet: ...which is roughly three times longer than cells growing under aerobic conditions in analogous media (3.42 h, µ = 0.20).
growth_records.notes: Mu (0.20 h^-1) and doubling time (3.42 h) directly reported for aerobic growth in 'analogous media' (comparison condition to the anaerobic acetate/iodate experiment). Exact aerobic medium composition (e.g., whether iodate/molybdate were omitted) is not restated verbatim at this point in the text, so composition is assumed identical to the base minimal medium recipe minus iodate; confidence downgraded to medium for the composition field only (rate values themselves are explicitly stated).
